title: Tests reimplement the app inline and never exercise app.py
evidence: tests/test_api.py:14 create_test_app() duplicates the entire Flask app; grep shows no `import app`/`from app`, so app.py has no direct coverage (test_coverage=0.65 from scores.json)
suggestion: Import the routes/factory from app.py in the tests so the production code under test is the code that ships

title: App runs with debug=True bound to 0.0.0.0
evidence: app.py:213 app.run(debug=True, host='0.0.0.0', port=5000) exposes the Werkzeug interactive debugger on all interfaces
suggestion: Default debug to False (or read from env) and bind to 127.0.0.1 unless external access is intended

title: No requirements.txt; dependencies installed ad hoc
evidence: no requirements.txt/pyproject.toml in run_dir; README.md:24 instructs `pip install flask` manually
suggestion: Add a requirements.txt pinning flask so the environment is reproducible

title: PUT uses full-replace semantics (title+author required on update)
evidence: app.py:171 update_book calls validate_book, so a partial update sending only {year} returns 400; test_update_book_invalid_year (tests/test_api.py:356) sends {year:'invalid'} and passes on the missing-title branch, not the year check
suggestion: If partial updates are desired, merge with the existing record; otherwise document the full-replace contract
